rationale.R1.6: The delegation is exclusively 'Polish this' — the task is entirely sentence/paragraph quality: eliminating hedging redundancy ('may potentially', 'could possibly', 'we believe reflects'), removing repetitive boilerplate ('which we believe reflects a modest improvement over the baseline' appears three times verbatim), tightening prose rhythm, and achieving professional register appropriate for a results section.
rationale.RX.1: Polishing must preserve all epistemic hedges that are scientifically warranted (e.g., seed variance, low-data regime caveats) while eliminating purely stylistic over-hedging; distinction requires active tracking.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains stacked hedges ('may potentially', 'could possibly') that, if collapsed too aggressively during polish, risk flipping into overclaim — the rewrite must calibrate hedge strength to the actual evidence presented.
rationale.RX.3: Scope is strictly prose improvement of this results paragraph; structural reorganization, novelty framing, and citation work are all out of scope and must not be introduced.
rationale.RX.4: Results concern clinical variant effect prediction; polished language that inadvertently sharpens claims beyond what the data support could mislead downstream clinical or research users.
rationale.RX.5: All numeric values (Spearman correlations, panel names, baseline identifiers) must be preserved verbatim and traceable back to the source artifact.
